diagrams.title: Virtual Function Call Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how a virtual function call resolves at runtime through vtable
diagrams.purpose: Understand late binding mechanism and how object type determines function execution, not pointer type

diagrams.title: Hierarchical Function Resolution
diagrams.description: Demonstrates that virtual functions resolve to the most derived class implementation
elements: Arrow showing 'Call goes to bottom-most implementation'
Pointer/reference at top pointing to actual object
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the hierarchical nature - function resolution follows inheritance chain to most derived (bottom) class

diagrams.title: Diamond Problem and Solution
diagrams.description: Shows diamond inheritance without and with virtual inheritance
elements: WITHOUT virtual: two separate base subobjects (duplicated)
WITH virtual: single shared base subobject
diagrams.purpose: Understand virtual inheritance's role in eliminating ambiguity and ensuring single base instance

diagrams.title: Object Slicing Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows what happens when derived object assigned to base by value
elements: Large derived class object box with extra fields
Resulting base object box (smaller, missing derived parts)
diagrams.purpose: Understand why pass-by-value destroys polymorphism and causes data loss

diagrams.title: vtable Structure per Class
diagrams.description: Illustrates vtable layout for base and derived classes
elements: Base Class vtable: Base::func1 ptr, Base::func2 ptr
Derived Class vtable: Derived::func1 ptr (overridden), Base::func2 ptr (inherited)
vptr in each object pointing to respective vtable
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how different classes have different vtables and how overriding replaces entries
